constraints: Warm-up and activation must be completed before any heavy work (5–10 minutes light aerobic plus 2–3 activation drills).
Progress load only if you can complete the work within the target rep range with control for 2–3 sessions; use small increases.
Prioritize form over load; stop increasing load if technique degrades.
Use RPE as a guide (mostly sets at RPE 7–8); reserve heavier sets for a controlled top set only.
Include single-leg work and posterior chain work to balance knee and hip forces.
Deload when performance drops or soreness persists (reduce volume ~30% for a week).
Knee safety: prefer goblet or split squats before heavy bilateral squats; ensure knee tracks in line with toes and maintain heel contact; avoid painful ranges.
Lower-back safety: master hip hinge, maintain neutral spine, brace and breathe, hinge from hips, and start with dumbbells/trap bar to reduce lumbar shear; avoid fast jerks.
Mobility integration: fold mobility into daily warm-ups and cooldowns (5–7 minutes) and include one 8–12 minute focused mobility session 1–2 times per week; aim for ~35–50 minutes of mobility weekly.
Mobility should directly support lifts (hips, thoracic spine, ankles, shoulders) with clear carryover.
Keep mobility time-efficient; do not add excessive weekly time; target ~35–50 minutes per week for mobility.
decisions: Adopt double-progression as the primary progression method (reps first, then weight).
If weight increments are limited, progress by adding 1–2 reps per set until you reach the top of the target range.
Use block periodization: 4–6 weeks of heavier, lower-rep work followed by 4–6 weeks of moderate reps.

Add an extra training day only after progressing consistently and with adequate recovery; ensure the extra day is complementary (e.g., technique, mobility, or accessory work) rather than duplicating a heavy day.
Schedule a deload week every 4–8 weeks if fatigue accumulates (reduce volume ~30%).
Swap 1–2 dumbbell main lifts for barbell equivalents when gym access is available; keep dumbbells for accessory work.
Provide a downloadable travel workout as a deliverable.
entities: entity: Barbell back squat (and front squat) priority over dumbbell squats in gym
entity: Deadlift variants (conventional barbell, trap-bar) provide heavy posterior-chain loading
entity: Barbell bench press (or barbell incline) for higher load and stable progression
entity: Heavy barbell rows / T‑bar rows
entity: Trap bar carries / heavy carries and sled work
entity: Cable machines and pulleys (lat pulldown, cable rows, face pulls)
entity: Isolation machines for hypertrophy or rehab (leg press, leg curl, leg extension, chest fly)
open: Integrate rest days and active recovery guidelines into the 4-week dumbbell plan and provide a sample weekly schedule.
Develop and provide 4 compact 30-minute templates (A–D) with warm-up and cooldown suitable for different priorities; offer to tailor to a specific day (e.g., heavy lower or upper focus).
Provide a 4-week rotation of enjoyable workout ideas tailored to the user's schedule.
Provide a downloadable 20-minute travel workout for phone save.
